entry.title: Does Colorism or Colour-based variation exist in India?

link.title: Does Colorism or Colour-based variation exist in India?

content.p: I am a Bachelor's in Computer Science and Engineering student developing a taste for avid learning, thinking, coding, reading, clicking pictures, writing blogs, eating and listening to a spectrum of music.

Currently, I am working as a research assistant at the Indian Statistical Institute on Cognitive science problems using Computer vision techniques. The fact that how a brain does what it does excites me and lures me into going root deep into the problems. One of the challenges that we face in today's world is colourism (colour based variation) on different perspectives and matters. One such imposition is done on film industry where cast members are selected on strict parameters, one being the skin tone or precisely you may say fair skin tone is selected over the average leftovers thereby producing a gulf in between the leftover common mass of people and the prioritized people.
One such challenge is being explored on my paper presented on Cambridge University Press. If you're interested to know about the insights of my work please do comment below.

div.style: position: relative; padding-bottom: 56.25%; height: 0; overflow: hidden;

iframe.style: position: absolute; top: 0; left: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%; border:0;
iframe.title: YouTube Video

content.h3: Want to know more about me?

entry.summary:
I am a Bachelor's in Computer Science and Engineering student developing a taste for avid learning, thinking, coding, reading, clicking pictures, writing blogs, eating and listening to a spectrum of music.
Currently, I am working as a research assistant at the Indian Statistical Institute on Cognitive science problems using Computer vision techniques. The fact that how a brain does what it does excites me and lures me into going root deep into the problems. One of the challenges that we face in today's world is colourism (colour based variation) on different perspectives and matters. One such imposition is done on film industry where cast members are selected on strict parameters, one being the skin tone or precisely you may say fair skin tone is selected over the average leftovers thereby producing a gulf in between the leftover common mass of people and the prioritized people.
One such challenge is being explored on my paper presented on Cambridge University Press. If you're interested to know about the insights of my work please do comment below.
